name: Chocolate Baklava
recipeInstructions:
Preparation Time: 1:30
1. In a small skillet, melt butter over low heat. Let stand a few minutes. Skim off and discard any white foam from the top and do not use any milky residue that sinks to the bottom. The clear yellow liquid is clarified butter.
2. Preheat oven to 350°F. In a medium bowl, combine pecans, cinnamon, 1/4 cup sugar, and chocolate chips; mix well. Open and stack filo sheets on a damp towel. Cover with a damp towel to prevent drying out. Brush a 12 x 18-inch sheet cake pan with clarified butter.
3. Using a sharp knife, cut the pastry into 50 diamond-shaped pieces. Do not remove the pastry from the pan. Brush the top with remaining clarified butter. Bake for 40 to 50 minutes, or until golden.
4. About 30 minutes before the baklava is finished baking, in a small saucepan, combine the remaining 1/2 cup sugar and orange juice. Heat to boiling over medium heat, stirring often. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 10 minutes. Stir in vanilla. Pour hot orange syrup over the baked baklava.
5. When the baklava is cool, drizzle Thin Chocolate Glaze on top. Allow glaze to set up before serving. Baklava may be kept, covered, at room temperature for 4 to 5 days. Freeze for longer storage.
Thin Chocolate Glaze:
2 (1-ounce) squares Semisweet chocolate
1 Tablespoon Butter
In a small glass bowl, combine chocolate, butter, and 1 tablespoon water. Heat in microwave on High for 30 to 45 seconds, or until melted and smooth when stirred.
